title: Explicit zero-free regions for the Riemann zeta-function

thm:1.1.summary: Headline explicit Vinogradov–Korobov zero-free region: no zeros of $\zeta(\sigma+it)$ for $|t|\ge 3$ and $\sigma \ge 1 - \frac{1}{55.241(\log|t|)^{2/3}(\log\log|t|)^{1/3}}$ — improving Ford's constant $57.54$.

thm:1.2.summary: Asymptotic VK zero-free region: for sufficiently large $|t|$, no zeros with $\sigma \ge 1 - \frac{1}{48.1588(\log|t|)^{2/3}(\log\log|t|)^{1/3}}$ — the asymptotic constant improving Ford's $0.05507$ to $0.05617776$.

thm:1.3.summary: Explicit classical-type zero-free region: no zeros for $|t|\ge 2$ and $\sigma \ge 1 - \frac{1}{5.558691\,\log|t|}$ — improving Ford's classical constant $8.464$.

thm:1.4.summary: Intermediate zero-free region: no zeros for $|t|\ge \exp(1000)$ and $\sigma > 1 - \frac{0.05035}{h(t)} + \frac{0.0349}{h^2(t)}$ with $h(t)=\frac{27}{164}\log|t| + 7.096$; bridges the classical and VK ranges.

lem:4.1.summary: Zero-detector for $\zeta$: for $s=1+it$ and almost all $\eta>0$, $-\Re\frac{\zeta'}{\zeta}(s)$ equals a $\cosh^{-2}$-weighted log-integral plus a localized cotangent sum over zeros. Stated as a direct application of Ford's Lemma 2.2.

lem:4.2.summary: Laplace-transform lemma for a non-negative compactly-supported $f$ with $F_0(z)=F(z)-f(0)/z$ satisfying $|F_0(z)|\le D/|z|^2$; obtained by combining Ford's Lemmas 4.5 and 4.6.

lem:4.3.summary: Growth-to-integral bridge: a $\zeta$ growth bound $|\zeta(\sigma+i\tau)|\le X t^Y \log^Z t$ (with $Y+Z>0.1$) yields an explicit integral bound; quoted from Ford's Lemma 3.4.

lem:4.4.summary: Degree-$K$ trigonometric positivity: for non-negative trig-polynomial coefficients $b_j$, $\int_{-\infty}^\infty \frac{1}{\cosh^2 u}\sum_{j=1}^K b_j \log|\zeta(1+\eta+ijt_1+iut_2)|\,du \ge -2b_0\log\zeta(1+\eta)$; generalizes Ford's Lemma 5.1 to degree $K$.

lem:4.5.summary: Zero-counting bound: for $0<\eta\le 1/4$, $t\ge 100$, $N(t,\eta) \le 1.3478\eta^{3/2}B\log t + 0.479 + \frac{\log A - \log\eta + \frac23\log\log t}{1.879}$; the corrected form of Ford's Lemma 4.2 (fixing a constant in the hypothesis range).

lem:4.6.summary: Sum-over-zeros bound on $\sum_{|1+it-\rho|\ge\eta}\frac{1}{|1+it-\rho|^2}$ for $t\ge 10000$, with sharpened constants; the improved analogue of Ford's Lemma 4.3, propagating the corrected $N(t,\eta)$ constant.

lem:4.7.summary: The detection step: for a putative zero $\beta+it$ with $1-\beta\le\eta/2$ and a zero-free rectangle of width $\lambda$, derives the contradiction-yielding inequality the explicit constants are optimized over; assembles the zero detector, the degree-$K$ trig polynomial and the sum-over-zeros bound.

lem:6.1.summary: Medium-range engine for Theorem 1.4: for a zero $\beta+it$ with $t\ge\exp(1000)$, $\beta\ge 1-1712^{-1}$ and a zero-free rectangle, yields a $\lambda$-inequality; follows Ford's Lemma 9.2 with a better $J(t)$, a better trigonometric polynomial and a higher effective height.

edges.note: Section 5's proof of the explicit VK region applies the detection step (Lemma 4.7).
The asymptotic VK constant is read off from the same detection step (Lemma 4.7).
Section 7's proof of the classical-type region uses the detection step (Lemma 4.7).
Section 6 proves the intermediate region from the medium-range Lemma 6.1.
The explicit VK constant is optimized over the degree-$K$ trigonometric polynomial (Lemma 4.4).
The detection step is built on the zero-detector identity (Lemma 4.1).
The detection step uses the degree-$K$ trigonometric positivity inequality (Lemma 4.4).
The detection step uses the sum-over-zeros bound (Lemma 4.6).
The sum-over-zeros bound uses the zero count $N(t,\eta)$ from Lemma 4.5.
The medium-range engine uses an improved trigonometric polynomial (Lemma 4.4).
The medium-range argument reuses the zero-detector mechanism (Lemma 4.1).

main_results.description: Four explicit zero-free regions of record: VK-type (1.1), asymptotic VK (1.2), classical-type (1.3), and the intermediate region (1.4).

imported_machinery.description: The Ford (2002/arXiv 1910.08205) detection apparatus, re-quoted and re-optimized with sharpened explicit constants: zero detector, Laplace lemma, growth-to-integral bridge, degree-$K$ trig positivity, zero counting, sum over zeros.

detection_step.description: The assembled inequality (Lemma 4.7) over which the explicit constants of Theorems 1.1–1.3 are optimized.

medium_range.description: The medium-range engine (Lemma 6.1) behind the intermediate region (Theorem 1.4).

Ford2000.title: Zero-free regions for the Riemann zeta function (K. Ford)
